question: Write a simple C++ calculator program. It should ask the user for two numbers and an arithmetic operator (+, -, *, /). Use a `switch` statement to perform the chosen operation and display the result. Handle division by zero appropriately.

test_cases.expected:
Welcome to the Simple Calculator!
Please enter the first number: Please enter an operator (+, -, *, /): Please enter the second number: Result: 15

Welcome to the Simple Calculator!
Please enter the first number: Please enter an operator (+, -, *, /): Please enter the second number: Result: 13

Welcome to the Simple Calculator!
Please enter the first number: Please enter an operator (+, -, *, /): Please enter the second number: Result: 24

Welcome to the Simple Calculator!
Please enter the first number: Please enter an operator (+, -, *, /): Please enter the second number: Result: 5

Welcome to the Simple Calculator!
Please enter the first number: Please enter an operator (+, -, *, /): Please enter the second number: Error: Division by zero is not allowed.

Welcome to the Simple Calculator!
Please enter the first number: Invalid input. Please enter a valid number.
Please enter the first number: Please enter an operator (+, -, *, /): Please enter the second number: Result: 15

Welcome to the Simple Calculator!
Please enter the first number: Please enter an operator (+, -, *, /): Please enter the second number: Error: Invalid operator.

Welcome to the Simple Calculator!
Please enter the first number: Please enter an operator (+, -, *, /): Please enter the second number: Invalid input. Please enter a valid number.
Please enter the second number: Result: 25
